items.q: Does buying in bulk always save a business money overall?
items.correct: Bulk-buying lowers the price per unit, but it raises storage costs, ties up cash and risks waste if demand falls — the net effect depends on the product.
items.misconception: Bigger orders are always cheaper for the business overall.
items.random_wrong: Bulk buying is taxed at a higher rate.
items.why: Students stop at the unit-price discount and miss the holding-cost trade-offs.

items.q: Does higher productivity mean employees are working longer hours?
items.correct: No — productivity is output per worker per time period; it rises through better training, equipment and methods, not necessarily longer hours.
items.misconception: The only way to raise productivity is to make staff work more hours.
items.random_wrong: Productivity measures how tidy the factory is.
items.why: Confusing effort and hours with efficiency hides the role of capital and training.

items.q: Is quality the same as luxury?
items.correct: No — quality means consistently meeting customer expectations and being fit for purpose; a budget product can be high quality if it does its job reliably.
items.misconception: Only expensive products can be high quality.
items.random_wrong: Quality is decided by the size of the advertising budget.
items.why: Defining quality as fitness for purpose unlocks questions on quality control at all price points.

items.q: Which method best suits a bespoke wedding dress?
items.why: It's Job production. “Batch production” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: Output rises faster than inputs. “Output falls and inputs rise” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: 5 workers each work 8 hours and produce 400 units total. What is productivity, in units per worker per hour?
items.why: The real figure is 10. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
